item.title: To dot, or not to dot, that is the question
item.description: A look at the day ahead in European and global markets from Stella Qiu

item.title: Nippon Steel secures $5.7 billion of loans for U.S. Steel takeover
item.description: Nippon Steel has вҖӢsecured loans totalling вҖҢ900 billion yen ($5.67 billion) from the вҖӢJapan Bank вҖӢfor International Cooperation (JBIC) вҒ and major Japanese вҖӢprivate lenders вҖӢfor its acquisition of U.S. Steel, it вҖӢsaid in вҖӢa statement on Wednesday.

item.title: Microsoft weighs legal action over $50 billion Amazon-OpenAI cloud deal, FT reports
item.description: Microsoft is вҖӢweighing legal action against вҖҢAmazon and OpenAI over a $50 billion deal that вҖӢcould breach Microsoft's exclusive вҖӢcloud partnership with the вҒ ChatGPT maker, the Financial вҖӢTimes reported on Wednesday.

item.title: Fed likely to hold rates steady as Iran war shocks policy debate
item.description: Federal Reserve officials, convening in a wartime setting that began less than three weeks ago, are expected to hold interest rates steady on Wednesday, but more significantly to outline in a new policy statement and projections how they вҖӢfeel President Donald Trump's decision to launch an open-ended conflict with Iran has recast the outlook for the U.S. economy, inflation and monetary policy.

item.title: Chipmakers in Malaysia monitoring risks from helium supply disruptions, association says
item.description: Semiconductor firms in Malaysia are monitoring risks from disruptions to helium supplies due to the conflict in the Middle вҖӢEast, though the situation has not caused any operational interruptions so вҖҢfar, an industry executive told Reuters.

item.title: A mystery AI model has developers buzzing: Is this DeepSeek's latest blockbuster?
item.description: A powerful artificial intelligence model that appeared anonymously on a developer platform last week has sparked speculation that Chinese startup DeepSeek may be quietly testing its next-generation system ahead of an official launch.

item.title: Samsung Elec plans to produce Tesla chips starting late 2027
item.description: Samsung Electronics вҖӢsaid on Wednesday вҖҢthat it expects to вҖӢstart вҖӢvolume production of Tesla's вҒ chips вҖӢat Samsung's factory вҖӢin Texas in the second вҖӢhalf of вҖӢnext year.

item.title: Asian stocks rally as oil retreats, Fed in spotlight
item.description: Asian shares rallied on Wednesday as oil prices paused their gains, with markets turning to the U.S. Federal Reserve meeting to see вҖӢhow policymakers will balance growth and inflation risks amid ongoing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East.

item.title: Elliott Management confirms stake in Japan shipper Mitsui OSK
item.description: Elliott вҖӢInvestment Management вҖҢsaid on вҖӢWednesday вҖӢthat it вҒ has вҖӢa built вҖӢa "significant" investment in вҖӢJapanese вҖӢshipping company вҖҢMitsui вҒ OSK Lines , confirming вҖӢan вҖӢearlier Reuters вҒ report.

item.title: Chinese authorities approve Nvidia's H200 AI chip sales, source says
item.description: Chinese authorities have granted approval for multiple Chinese companies вҖӢto purchase H200 AI chips from Nvidia, вҖҢaccording to a person familiar with the situation.

item.title: AMD CEO Lisa Su to visit Samsung chip plant in South Korea, discuss expanding ties, source says
item.description: Advanced Micro Devices CEO Lisa Su is set to вҖӢvisit Samsung Electronics' chip production site вҖҢin Pyeongtaek, South Korea on Wednesday, where she will tour production lines and discuss вҖӢexpanding cooperation beyond memory into вҖӢfoundry manufacturing, a source with knowledge вҒ of the trip said.

item.title: Oil prices drop as U.S. crude inventories show an increase
item.description: Oil prices slightly fell on Wednesday morning after sources citing American Petroleum Institute figures showed an вҖӢincrease in U.S. crude inventories.

item.title: Samsung sees AI driving strong chip demand in 2026, executive says
item.description: Samsung Electronics вҖӢsees strong demand вҖҢfor chips continuing this year, driven вҖӢby the вҖӢglobal artificial intelligence wave, вҒ but rising вҖӢmemory chip prices could вҖӢhit computer and mobile shipments, its top вҖӢexecutive said вҖӢon Wednesday.

item.title: Trump administration defends Anthropic blacklisting in US court
item.description: The Trump administration said in a Tuesday court filing that the PentagonвҖҷs blacklisting of Anthropic was justified and lawful, opposing вҖӢthe artificial intelligence labвҖҷs high-stakes lawsuit challenging the decision.

item.title: Dollar holds losses as risk appetite flickers ahead of central bank meetings
item.description: The dollar held losses on Wednesday as a glimmer of risk appetite came back to markets ahead of a slate of key central bank decisions.

item.title: With watchful eye on Iran war, crypto dodges UAE disruption
item.description: Laia FernГЎndez has been working as normal as a crypto marketing executive from her high-rise apartment in Dubai's gleaming downtown business district, even as occasional sounds вҖӢof projectile interceptions act as reminders of the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran.

item.title: Musk's motives are debated as Twitter shareholder trial nears end
item.description: A lawyer for former Twitter shareholders suing Elon Musk urged a federal jury on Tuesday to вҖӢhold the world's richest person liable for driving down Twitter's stock price in 2022 by attempting to back out of or вҖҢrenegotiate his $44 billion purchase of the social media platform.

item.title: Brazilian truckers weigh strike as diesel prices jump amid Middle East conflict
item.description: Truck drivers' unions in Brazil are advocating for a strike as early as this week after the recent jump in вҖӢdiesel prices due to the conflict in the Middle East, вҖҢa union head said on Tuesday.

item.title: Fintech lender SoFi disputes short seller Muddy Waters' report, considers legal action
item.description: California-based вҖӢfintech lender SoFi Technologies said on вҖҢTuesday that it may explore legal action against short seller Muddy Waters Research, calling вҖӢits report "factually inaccurate and misleading."

item.title: Top Japan firms set to offer big pay hikes, focus on Iran conflict ahead
item.description: Many major companies are set to offer big pay hikes as they wrap up their annual wage вҖӢtalks with unions on Wednesday, reflecting strong pay momentum вҖҢfor a fourth consecutive year, although uncertainty from the Middle East conflict clouds the outlook.

item.title: BP locks out union workers at its Midwest refinery
item.description: BP on Tuesday barred about 800 вҖӢUnited Steelworkers members вҖҢfrom its 440,000 barrel-per-day refinery in Whiting, вҖӢIndiana starting at вҖӢ12:00 a.m. on March вҒ 19 after negotiations вҖӢwith the union broke вҖӢdown.

item.title: Exclusive: Nvidia preparing Groq chips that can be sold in Chinese market, sources say
item.description: Nvidia is preparing a вҖӢversion of its Groq artificial-intelligence chips that can be sold to вҖҢthe Chinese market, two sources familiar with the matter told Reuters on Tuesday.

item.title: US Postal Service could run out of money as soon as October
item.description: The head of the U.S. Postal Service told Congress on Tuesday it could run out of вҖӢmoney in October or November if the agency continues to make required вҖҢretirement and other payments to the government.

item.title: Amazon plans drastic cut in packages it sends through post office, WSJ reports
item.description: Amazon.com is looking to sharply cut the вҖӢnumber of packages it вҖҢsends through the U.S. Postal Service, a move that вҖӢcould cost the agency вҖӢmillions of dollars in revenue, вҒ the Wall Street вҖӢJournal reported on Tuesday.

item.title: Australia's Woodside names Liz Westcott as new CEO
item.description: Australia's вҖӢWoodside вҖҢEnergy вҖӢnamed вҖӢLiz вҒ Westcott вҖӢas вҖӢits new вҖӢchief вҖӢexecutive officer вҖҢand вҒ managing вҖӢdirector вҖӢon вҒ Wednesday.

item.title: FTC monitoring how drug companies react to patent cliff, official says
item.description: The вҖӢU.S. Federal Trade Commission is on the lookout for anticompetitive practices in the pharmaceutical вҖҢindustry with a number of blockbuster drugs near the end of their patents, the FTC's head of antitrust enforcement said on Tuesday, as the agency looks to protect consumers from unfair prices.

item.title: US gasoline prices soar past $3.75 a gallon as Middle East war rages on
item.description: The U.S. national average price for gasoline surpassed $3.75 a gallon for the first time since October вҖӢ2023 on Tuesday, GasBuddy data showed, as global fuel вҖҢmarkets continue to reel under supply disruptions caused by the Middle East war.

item.title: BHP names Brandon Craig as CEO, succeeding Mike Henry
item.description: Mining giant BHP вҖӢGroup on Wednesday named Brandon Craig as вҖҢCEO to succeed Mike Henry, who will step down after more than six years at вҖӢthe helm.

item.title: Trading Day: Oil back above $100... and so?
item.description: Stocks rose, and bond yields and the dollar slipped on Tuesday, as investors shrugged off a rebound in oil prices that lifted Brent crude back above $100 a barrel, and turned their attention to the Federal Reserve's interest rate decision on Wednesday.

item.title: US securities regulator issues long-awaited crypto guidance
item.description: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission on Tuesday issued an interpretation clarifying which types of cryptocurrencies are considered securities and how a "non-security" вҖӢdigital asset could meet certain conditions to become an investment contract.
